job_snippets: Build performant, accessible React or Next.js applications with TypeScript; manage state with Redux, Zustand, or React Query.
Implement responsive, pixel-perfect UIs from Figma designs using Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, or styled-components.
Write unit and integration tests with Jest, React Testing Library, and end-to-end tests with Playwright or Cypress.
Optimize web performance: Core Web Vitals, lazy loading, code splitting, image optimization, and caching strategies.
Consume REST and GraphQL APIs; handle authentication flows (OAuth, JWT) and error states gracefully.
Build reusable component libraries and design systems; publish to npm or internal registries.
Configure Webpack, Vite, or Turbopack; understand module bundling, tree-shaking, and build optimization.
Implement SEO best practices: meta tags, structured data, Open Graph, sitemap generation.
Collaborate with designers and backend engineers in agile teams; participate in code reviews.
Use Git workflows: feature branches, pull requests, rebasing, and semantic versioning.
Implement internationalization (i18n) and localization for multi-language applications.
Work with animations and transitions using Framer Motion, GSAP, or CSS animations.
Deploy to Vercel, Netlify, or AWS CloudFront; manage environment configs and preview deployments.
Understand browser APIs: LocalStorage, Service Workers, WebSockets, Intersection Observer.
Implement accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1): ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, screen reader testing.
Work with monorepos using Nx, Turborepo, or Lerna for large-scale frontend projects.
Profile and debug performance using Chrome DevTools, Lighthouse, and bundle analyzers.
Integrate analytics and monitoring: Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Sentry for error tracking.
Experience with micro-frontends, module federation, or component-driven development with Storybook.
Strong understanding of HTTP, web security (CSP, CORS, XSS, CSRF), and browser rendering pipeline.
